backend: K=4 ensemble probe-as-loss; LARGER N (test ~198); per-seed paired bootstrap CI on the disjoint audit delta
verdict: CEILING EFFECT, not low power -> w5 stays OPEN at 2/3 significant. Larger N (test ~198) confirms 2/3 seeds' audit-delta CI EXCLUDES 0 (seed0 0.889->0.975 CI[+0.046,+0.126]; seed1 0.753->0.950 CI[+0.141,+0.258]) but seed2 STILL grazes 0 (0.919->0.939 CI[-0.030,+0.071]). Larger N did NOT close seed2 because its non-significance is a CEILING effect: seed2's base audit is already 0.919 (near-saturated), so the room to improve -- and thus the delta (+0.02) -- is small. The improvement MAGNITUDE scales with headroom: seed1's low base 0.753 -> +0.197, high-base seeds -> small +. This substantiates the K=4 ensemble as a genuine anti-Goodhart method (it improves the disjoint audit whenever there is headroom, and never degrades it -- unlike v1 single-probe which GAMED it), and honestly explains the seed heterogeneity. A clean 3/3 would need seeds with MATCHED moderate base-audit (headroom).
